editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me reply to a DM that says: "How did we even cross paths online?"

Sure—here are a few options you can paste:

1) "I think we came across each other through Instagram—maybe via a mutual connection or a post. Glad we connected either way."
2) "Pretty sure your profile popped up in my feed/explore and your work caught my eye."
3) "I don’t remember the exact moment, but I’m really happy we connected—curious what sparked it on your side?"
Make it shorter and a bit playful, but not flirty.

Options:
- "Somewhere in the internet universe—pretty sure Instagram did its thing. Glad we crossed paths."
- "Instagram algorithm magic, I think. Happy it connected us."
- "Not 100% sure—probably IG doing what it does. What brought you to my page?"

"Interstellar" means “between stars.” "Intersphere" isn’t a standard word—people sometimes use it jokingly for “the internet” (similar vibe to “the online sphere”).

Fake-not-about-you trivia: NASA’s Voyager 1 is in interstellar space, and the word “interstellar” got a popularity boost from the 2014 film *Interstellar*.
Need a quick reply for IG DM: they asked if I’m free to hop on a 15-min call tomorrow, but I’m in back-to-back meetings until 3pm CET.

You can keep it friendly and specific about timing. For example:

“Hey! Thanks for reaching out—happy to chat. I’m booked in meetings until 3pm CET tomorrow, but I’m free after that. Would 3:30pm CET work for a quick 15-min call?”
Small correction: that person didn’t DM me on Instagram. We’ve been talking over LinkedIn messages — I just copied the text into Instagram by mistake.

Got it — so the actual channel is LinkedIn messages, not Instagram DMs. If you want, you can reply with something like: “I believe we connected on LinkedIn—likely through mutual interests or a shared contact. Glad we did.”
